session.notes: Session contains 13 slides and 3 sections after velocity_commit_deck.

scores.notes: The engine handled weighted analysis, deck build, export, and session persistence correctly on a 654-variable dataset.
The intended MCP path completed cleanly end-to-end; the main remaining drag is discovery churn rather than tool transport friction.
Topic discovery was inconsistent and category-level discovery was weak, especially for NHS, trust, and demographic-break queries.
The run produced a coherent 13-slide deck and matching session handoff artifact.
Weight detection was good, but the product still lacked strong break-variable guidance and theme-narrowing support.
The brief and playbook supported the run; the main bottleneck was search quality, not prompt framing.

notableFindings: The MCP path now supports a full weighted BSA workflow: load, discover, analyze, build deck, commit deck, export PPTX, and export session.
EU referendum vote shows strong age, party, education, and class gradients, with all four core crosstabs highly significant.
NHS satisfaction varies sharply by age and party but not materially by income in this run (p = 0.061756536921150795 for HHIncQ).
Government trust remains broadly low, with especially strong party differences and weaker age effects.
blockers: Semantic search ranked incorrect top hits for NHS, trust, and demographic queries, so discovery still required manual judgment.
Large-survey discovery still lacks product-level affordances for break-variable selection and theme narrowing.
followUps: Add category-aware or intent-aware search affordances for demographic and break-variable discovery.
Surface recommended breaks and starter analyses once a substantive topic variable is selected.
Clarify the chi-square field shape in docs and examples so agents read stats objects correctly.
Freeze this run as the current MCP baseline for BSA-scale deck and session generation.
